summary: P0 content + bookmarks testing complete. Backend: 55/55 pytest passing (16 auth regression + 39 new content/bookmark tests). Frontend: end-to-end admin authoring works for all 4 content types, memo add/delete works, universal bookmarks across all 4 types reach the /saved page with type filters, dashboard KPIs reflect real counts, member view correctly hides admin buttons.

minor.issue: Duplicate ticker triggers Mongo DuplicateKeyError but is not caught -> returns 500. Should return 409/400 with friendly message.

Members are allowed to CREATE a bookmark on a draft research/education/report (only the GET /api/bookmarks list filters drafts out). For consistency POST should also 404 if content is unpublished for non-admin.

ui_bugs.issue: Invalid period (e.g. 2026/01) is submitted to backend which returns 422; the dialog stays open but [data-testid=editor-error] remains empty so the user gets no visible feedback about why the save failed. Add client-side regex validation or surface the 422 detail.
React console warning: 'Missing Description or aria-describedby for DialogContent'. Accessibility nit — add a <DialogDescription> or aria-describedby.

action_items: Surface a visible error in ContentEditorDialog when backend returns 422 for invalid report period (currently editor-error is empty).
Catch DuplicateKeyError in POST /api/companies and return 409 with 'Ticker already exists' instead of letting it 500.
Optionally reject member POST /api/bookmarks for unpublished content (currently the bookmark row is created but hydration filters it out, leaving an inert row in the DB).
Add DialogDescription / aria-describedby to all shadcn Dialog content blocks to clear the a11y warning.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py 695 lines — already close to the 700 line soft limit; consider splitting routes into modules (auth, content, companies, bookmarks) before adding more endpoints.

_published_filter is called inside list_education but list_education then re-builds the same query manually instead of calling _list_generic — duplication that drifted: education uses ['title','summary','body','track'] search fields. OK for now but extract.
Companies endpoint hides memos in list response by deleting after serialization — fine, but querying with projection would be cleaner and lighter on large memo arrays.
ReportIn uses pattern via Field; FastAPI will return 422 with location info but the frontend dialog isn't rendering it (see ui bug).

success_rate.frontend: 95% — all critical flows pass; only minor UX/a11y issues

test_credentials: Admin: admin@hamptoncrest.com / Hampton#2026 (verified working). Member registrations created with random emails like test_member_*@example.com and member_*@test.com.
seed_data_creation: Tests create TEST_ / UI_ prefixed data and clean most of it via DELETE. A handful of UI-created records (UI_R_*, UI_E_*, UTC*) remain because the test script did not delete them — main agent may seed/clear the demo content collections if a pristine DB is required.
context_for_next_testing_agent: test_content.py covers role gating, full CRUD for research/education/reports/companies, period validation, year filter, category/tag filter, search, memo add/delete, bookmark check/add/list/delete, idempotent bookmark, draft hydration filtering. test_auth.py (16) and test_content.py (39) both green against the preview URL. UI flow caveats: editor-category / editor-track / company-sector are <select> elements — use page.select_option(), not page.fill(). Company key_metrics list starts empty — click [data-testid=add-metric-button] N times to get N rows. For research/education/reports list items, use the <a> children of [data-testid=research-list] etc. rather than a free-text selector to avoid clicking duplicate matches in sidebars/dashboards.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — no critical issues. Two minor product issues identified: (1) report editor swallows backend 422 — the ContentEditorDialog catches the fetch error but isn't extracting `detail` from the FastAPI response into editor-error; (2) Mongo unique-index on company ticker is enforced at DB level but not wrapped in try/except so the user sees a generic 500. Both are 1-line fixes.
